query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "weavebench: a long-horizon, real-world benchmark for computer-use agen" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it directly measures the performance of Computer-Use Agents (CUAs) on long-horizon tasks, which is the core mechanism and domain of the user's question regarding the "WeaveBench" follow-up. It provides essential empirical baselines and alternative benchmarking approaches for the specific population of agents and task types (long-horizon, repetitive workflows) central to the user's research.
